author: Maghar Singh, Bhai
title: Dhann Kabir Bhagat
gloss: Verses in praise of Kabir, who, though only a weaver by birth, became a saint and founded a sect of his own.
notes: Kabir praised across the caste line ('only a weaver by birth') — the sant tradition's cross-communal appeal in Sikh-press verse.

author: Mansa Singh, Bhai
title: Bibri Biadh
gloss: A pamphlet on the evil effects of believing in the phenomena of possession by evil spirits.
notes: Reformist verse against belief in spirit-possession — rationalist Sikh-reform strand.

printer_verbatim: Civil and Military Organ Press, Ludhiána.
author: Muhammad Shah, M., Nizami, Marhum
title: Pittan Nama
gloss: A short poem in which the author condemns the evil practice of moaning and beating of the breast by the Indian ladies on the death of relatives.
notes: Muslim author (styled 'Marhum,' the late) writing Punjabi reform verse against ritual mourning (siyapa/pittan) — cross-community mourning-reform.

title: Nawan Qissa Duwale Nama
gloss: Verses condemning extravagance, gambling, and locking up money in the form of ornaments.
notes: Anti-ornament/anti-extravagance verse (cf. the Kairon anti-ornament campaign in Q3/Q4).

author: Partap Singh, Bhai
title: Siri Astotar Nankana Sahib
gloss: Verses in praise of Nankana Sahib, the birth place of Guru Nanak and a place of Sikh pilgrimage.
notes: Author dash-continuation of s24. Nankana Sahib devotion — the shrine central to the later (1921) Gurdwara Reform agitation.

author: Phuman Singh, Bhai
title: Kandhin Vasa arthat var Jorawar Singh wa Fate Singh Ji Chhote Sahib Jade Guru Gobind Singh Ji
gloss: A description of the tragic end of the two sons of Guru Gobind Singh who were buried alive.
publisher: The author, Assistant Station Master, Kot Kapura
notes: The martyrdom of the younger Sahibzadas (bricked alive at Sirhind) — railway employee author.

author: Phuman Singh, Bhai
title: Bara Masa Pakhand Khandan
gloss: This poem deals with the qualifications of a Guru or spiritual leader according to the Sikh Granth.
notes: Reg 383 — among the highest of the quarter, consistent with Q1 closing at ~384. Author dash-continuation of s26. Issue date 6 April 1910 (registered after quarter-end, as happens).
